blocks.content: Saturday night. The house was different on weekends — quieter in some ways, louder in others. Mr. Cole cooked on Saturdays. Real food, not the weeknight stuff.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: You got hit today.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: How do you know that?

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: You're walking stiff.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You notice everything.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: Someone has to.
Mr. Cole put a plate down. He didn't ask about practice. He never asked directly about things like that — he waited for you to bring it to him.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: You holding up?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Yeah. I went down twice. Got back up.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: How'd you get back up?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Same way as the first time. Just did it.
He nodded at that. Slow. Like it meant something he already knew but was glad to hear.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: That's the part that matters. Not how you fall. How you decide to get back up.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: I fell off my bike three times and I got back up every time.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: I know. I watched.
Larry Jr. looked at him like that was the best thing anyone had said to him in weeks.
I knew that feeling.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: Who's the girl?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: What?

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: You've been like this since Thursday. Walking slow. Thinking about something. It's a girl.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I don't know what you're talking about.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: You got a girlfriend you got a girlfriend —

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I do not have a girlfriend.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: You like somebody though.
He said it like it was obvious. He was seven.
Mr. Cole didn't say anything. Just kept eating. But he was listening — you could tell by the way he got a little more still.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: There's a girl in English class. She's — I don't know. She's different.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: Does she like you back?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I think so. Maybe.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: Then why don't you just tell her?
I didn't answer right away. And when I did I said something I hadn't planned to say.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I didn't actually think a girl like her would like me. Like — I saw it happening and part of me still didn't believe it was real.
The table was quiet.
Mr. Cole set his fork down. Just for a second. Then he picked it back up.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: That's worth paying attention to.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: What is?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Cole
blocks.content: The part of you that didn't believe it. That's not modesty. That's something you're going to have to work on.
He said it without judgment. Just like he said everything — clean, no extra.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: I would have just told her.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You're seven.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: So?
I didn't have an answer for that either.
